summary: Validated mobile 3D polish + desktop cinematic regression across 390/430/768/1920 viewports. All checks passed: content-first mobile layout, no horizontal scroll, 3D <canvas> mounts after deferred load, sticky CTA does not cover submit, desktop 400vh sticky scroll intact, sticky CTA hidden on desktop, UZ/RU toggle works on mobile & desktop.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx reads window.innerWidth at module init (useState initializer) — fine, but could hydrate mismatch if SSR is ever added.
SceneLoader shadows outer `t` (translations) with a setTimeout ref named `t`; harmless but stylistically ambiguous.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 verified mobile 3D polish + desktop regression. Findings: 390px hero: title bottom=266, CTA-telegram bottom=448, canvas top=620 (proper content-first ordering, no overlap). max scrollWidth = clientWidth at all viewports (390/430/768). Deferred 3D <canvas> mounts within ~2s. Sticky CTA at y=780 does not cover submit button (submit y+h fits above sticky). Desktop 1920 hero_section height=4320 (4x viewport = 400vh) preserved, sticky CTA hidden. UZ->RU->UZ toggle changes hero title text as expected. No issues to report.
